Hey, everyone. Julie here. Wanted to show you guys something really cool. So what you're looking at right here is a spreadsheet and it looks a lot more intimidating than it is. But I wanted to give you kind of behind the scenes on how I create info products. Now you guys are on mission five today, right? And basically you are being told to build all the things right here. Now we have the funnel Rolodex. The funnel Rolodex is an easy way to have other people do it. Some of you are going to be able to hire all the who's and get it done. Others of you are still going to need to create the raw content, okay? In fact, what you're looking at here, these are the raw worksheets that I created for this challenge. And then you can see over here, we had them designed. So this is a perfect example where I write out the content and someone does, goes and designs it. And that's kind of what the funnel Rolodex does. But even still, you might be a little overwhelmed because you have to actually build content. So I want to show you how I build content, how I create info products because I thought it might be helpful. And Google account, here we go. So I would use Google, all right? So we're going to go ahead and we're going to create a new folder and we're going to call it one funnel away challenge content, all right? This is the stuff that you're actually going to be creating. So once it's in here, the next thing I would do is take this link. I would grab a little icon. By the way, I use Chrome and you should too because you can create bookmarks, which will make it super easy. So go ahead and just drag it over to your bookmark bar right here. Whoops, I already have too many. Anyway, it'll appear like this. You just grab the little lock icon and pull it over. Then every single time you need to work on this and you only have a little bit of time, you can just grab the bookmark bar, boom, and there you go. Okay, you're ready to rock and roll. Okay, so next what we're going to do is if you have a bunch of different products, you're going to create a folder for each product. So let's say you're going to create flashcards and let's say you're going to create worksheets. Okay, so that's how you can organize it if you want or if it's just a few things, you can just keep it all in one folder. So I like to start with a Google Doc or a Google sheet or Google slide depends on what you're creating. But the very first thing I do is I write an outline and the outline just kind of shows me, all right, what's going to be in each of my products. So if you're doing a course, you're just going to do, you know, like this, something simple. All right, so you're going to do the outline of what are you going to teach. And then you want to know what will the student be able to do when they're done and what mode will it be delivered. So you just answered this like, okay, I'm going to teach how to play a C chord. What will the student be able to do? They'll be able to play a C chord. What mode will be delivered? It'll be a video. Okay, so go ahead and outline and then, you know, just make this your outline so that you know what it is that you're teaching. It should be super easy then to go ahead and do it. Now if you don't, I have created courses without using a fancy designer. And what I have done is simply created a Google doc and created almost like a template. You can do this. And so then all you need is to just make this a little template. So insert a header. Okay, and then you can upload your logo. You definitely should have the funnel roll at X for a logo. That's a terror. That's not actually a good logo. Hold on. Sorry. What do I have? I don't have any logos easily accessible. So I'll just grab something. Okay, let's just say that's my logo for now. Okay. And then insert a footer. And this is where you just put your, you know, your copyright 2018. All rights reserved, your name, et cetera, et cetera. Okay, like that. All right. Now here's your template. So every time you go to make a module, if you just do this once, you can do file, make a copy. You can do module one worksheet. Okay. It will copy everything and make it easy for you to not have to reinvent the wheel every time it'll be all ready to go for you. Okay. Don't discount how valuable audio files are. I sometimes will write out some notes and then I will just record an audio. I'm on a Mac. So I will go to quick time. Where is quick time? Right here. I will hit new audio recording. I'll start recording talk, blah, blah, blah, go through my notes, finish, save, right, module one and I will upload that right into my drive as well. Okay. So that's all there is to it. Now if you want to do like screen capture, which I'm doing right now, you can use something called screen flow. It's really not that hard. You see this little thing up here? Once you turn it on, you simply click there and hit record. It will record your screen. So if you have any sort of PowerPoint slides, something like this, you have your slide, you just go into presentation mode. And I think on screen flow, it's command shift to and it starts recording. You talk and then command shift to stops recording. And that's it. That's all there is to it. And once you have all of the raw stuff done, you can either hire a designer or if you just want to keep it simple, you can do it yourself. Even with something like PowerPoint presentation, if you create one like template, so you can use ones that are already built in, then you can just do file, make a copy for each additional module so that you can save all your settings. So those are just some few handy tips. Obviously, if you can afford to use the funnel Rolodex and you can afford a designer, please, please do so. Canva is another great tool. If you're trying to design things completely free, sign in with your Facebook account and you can create all kinds of things. You can create newsletters, you can create graphics, you can create logos. In fact, if you go up here and you look and you see like, okay, here is letter size, right? That would be great if you were doing some sort of newsletter. You can literally grab the template and just swap out your stuff and then you can hit add a new page. You can pull text in simply like this, add your text. I tend to just do it in docs, but if you're a super design, you want design, you can do that and go ahead, add a bunch of things and then just hit download. You're going to download it as a PDF. Simple. Now, the reason why this is costing me a dollar is because I have a premium element here and I'm pretty sure it's the picture in the back. So if I wanted to switch out the picture, I would just delete the picture and I could upload my own picture. So for example, I have a picture here of Russell, so I could upload that and stick it in there. Now when I go to download it, it's going to be entirely free. It will be downloaded and you can put it in the folder. Now, what I showed you at the beginning, this is what happens when you have multiple people working on content. Obviously, the mission, there's a lot of content. We've got the raw content and we've got the final content. We have the worksheet docs, the PDFs and we've got a lot of people working in here. And so I wanted to show you this. If you are the organized type and you like it, what I like to do when I'm beginning to create a suite of info products, which is really what the one funnel away challenge is, you can start by creating a Google Sheet. And the nice thing about Google Sheet is that everything has a link. So you can put here, you can put like products and then you can put like YouTube link, you can put worksheet link and you can set up your spreadsheet. Again, info product spreadsheet. Now you can again grab the link for this and put it on your bookmark bar. In fact, I would do the share, click it, get shareable link, change it to edit, copy that link and that can be the link for the bookmark bar. Okay, that can be the link up here. You can also, I think use just this one right here, just pull it on like this and it will open up this spreadsheet. So you have everything at your fingertips all the time. So you say, okay, so this is like the workbook. So I would say, let's see, workbook worksheet number one and it doesn't have a YouTube link, but it does have a link here and maybe we're going to use the PDF version of it or maybe we're going to use the doc version. So let's just pretend for a second that this worksheet is done. You can actually download it as a PDF when it's done. So if you design it, there it is as a PDF. You can upload it back into the drive so that you have the original file and you have the PDF version and then just right click and hit get shareable link, turn link sharing on, copy that and put it in there. Now what will happen is as you add more PDFs, more links, et cetera, et cetera, by the way, if you want it to not like flow over like that, you can hit text wrapping wrap or you can do text wrapping clip like that. I tend to like that. You add all your worksheets and basically everything is all stored and ready to go. So if you have someone from the funnel Rolodex that you want to design, you would probably wouldn't put the PDF links there because those are all designed, but you would give them. See how that does that? Isn't that pretty? You'd give them the raw files like this file right here. And so this would be the worksheet finals tab just like that. And maybe you have the worksheet raw. Put that there. If you have someone that you want to make, you know, to design it and you get their email address, you can send them this link and then they will do it. You can even insert a comment and just say, like I could talk to someone and say, Hey, Hey, this one is done and ready to be designed. So then you can assign it to that person just by hitting the little plus and then their email address that will go right to their email. When they open their email, it will take them right to this doc and then they can say, Oh, okay, this is ready to be designed. So I show you all of these things as completely optional. I just wanted to give you some behind the scenes of how it is that we pull off everything from video files to worksheets to resources to extra to the social posts that you're seeing to the copy and to the emails when we've got a team of seven or eight working and we're trying to be efficient. You guys are going to have to be efficient. You're going to have to do the best you can. Done is better than perfect. Okay.
